senses.gloss: to step along, to pace, to go, to depart, to travel, to walk

examples.text: مَنْ يَسِيرُ مَعَ ٱلْحُكَمَاءِ يَصِير حَكِيمًا.
examples.english: He who walks with the wise will be wise.

senses.gloss: to cause to depart, cause to travel
to function, work (properly), run, be in operation
to confide a secret to, to whisper in someone’s ear

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of the verb سَرَّ (sarra, “to gladden”).

Derived from the active participle of the verb سَرَى (sarā, “to circulate, to spread”).
